description:
sha1 + hex are used in exactly two non-test sites: task-ID generation (src/task.rs:127) and store-path hashing (src/store_paths.rs). For two Sha1::digest() calls the build pulls the full RustCrypto stack: sha1, digest, block-buffer, crypto-common, generic-array, typenum, cpufeatures, cfg-if (~8 crates).

WHY THIS IS A DECISION, NOT A SLAM-DUNK: balls already shells out to git everywhere, and 'git hash-object --stdin' IS the SHA-1 primitive the jq+git+ln philosophy points at — so on philosophy grounds this is the clearest current violation. But the honest trade-off is a subprocess fork per ID/path-hash vs. ~8 crates of well-vetted pure-Rust code. This task is to make and record that call explicitly, not to assume the cut.

SCOPE: (a) prototype replacing both call sites with a git hash-object invocation (reuse the existing git-command plumbing in src/git.rs; keep the hex string shape identical so task ids and store paths are unchanged); (b) confirm the RustCrypto chain fully drops from cargo tree and no behavior/format changes; (c) if the fork cost or determinism concerns outweigh the tree-purity gain, instead document the decision to KEEP sha1+hex as a deliberate, recorded exception (mirroring how the plugin/libc line is a deliberate kept exception) and close accordingly. Either outcome is a valid close; the deliverable is a defensible, written decision.
title: Decide: replace sha1+hex with git hash-object
